name: Progress Intent
summary: A determinate task/completion readout — how far a task has advanced toward done (profile % complete, course progress, a fundraising goal). Adopts the native `<progress>` / `role=progressbar` vocabulary verbatim (value, max; default max 1), and omitting `value` yields the indeterminate state (drops `aria-valuenow`). NO min and NO low/high/optimum zones — a position-in-a-known-range with zones is `meter`, and a discrete lifecycle state is `status-indicator` (three value-types, three homes by ARIA design; folding completion into a meter mis-types it). Optionally carries a generic secondary/comparison track — a second fill on the same axis (the native `<video>` `buffered` vs `currentTime` pattern, the YouTube/Netflix scrubber), scoped to generic completion and NEVER a trust/provenance signal. Decoupled from any pending/blocking lifecycle: `loader.progress` and flow-progress COMPOSE it as consumers, never its home. Ratified #1469 (Fork 2b); secondary track added #2533 (Fork 2). WE owns the intent, FUI the rendered block (constellation-placement).

presentation.description: How completion is drawn. `bar` is the native-first default (a horizontal track, the native `<progress>` shape); `circular` renders the same contract as a ring/arc (a presentation, never a separate intent — a spinner is the indeterminate circular presentation). The accessible `role=progressbar` + `aria-valuenow`/`aria-valuetext` are identical across presentations.

secondaryTrack.description: An optional second fill on the SAME track, alongside the primary completion fill — the native `<video>` `buffered` vs `currentTime` pattern (a lighter buffered range under the played fill; the everyday YouTube/Netflix scrubber). `none` (default) is a single completion fill. `comparison` renders a second GENERIC completion value on the same axis (buffered-vs-played, downloaded-vs-verified, estimated-vs-actual). CAVEAT — the second value is generic completion, NEVER a trust/data-provenance signal: the app's `claimed-vs-verified` distinction is a separate axis, a binding an app applies, never a term baked into this contract. This is a presentation dimension the intent owns, not a re-typing of `progress` into a provenance intent.

description:
What this is

A determinate task/completion readout: how far a task has advanced toward done — a profile's % complete, course progress, a fundraising goal. It adopts the native <progress> / role=progressbar contract verbatim: value and max (default 1). Omit value for the indeterminate state — the work is happening but its extent is unknown — which drops aria-valuenow (a spinner is the indeterminate circular presentation). Realizing build for the ratified #1469 placement call.

Not a meter, not a status

ARIA defines progressbar and meter as two separate roles by design: progress is task completion over time (may be indeterminate, no min, no zones), a meter is a position in [min,max] with low/high/optimum zones, and a status-indicator is a discrete lifecycle enum. Forcing a completion value through a meter mis-types it (per readout-placement-by-value-type).

Decoupled from the loading lifecycle

Progress is not a pending/blocking lifecycle — it is a pure completion magnitude. The loader intent's progress and a multi-step flow's progress compose this intent as consumers; this is its home, they are not. (The common case — a loading bar — is already covered by loader.progress, which is why this is low priority.)

Presentation

bar (default) is the native horizontal <progress> track. circular renders the identical contract as a ring/arc — a presentation, not a new intent. The accessible role=progressbar + aria-valuenow/aria-valuetext are identical across presentations.

Secondary / comparison track

Progress optionally carries a second fill on the same track — a native pattern, not a new intent. <video>'s buffered range under its currentTime is exactly this: every media player draws a lighter buffered fill beneath the played fill, and the YouTube/Netflix scrubber is its everyday form. Other occurrences: download managers (downloaded vs verified), torrent clients (have vs available), CI dashboards (estimated vs actual). Set secondaryTrack: comparison to render a second generic completion value on the same axis. Caveat (in the contract): the second track is generic completion, not a trust or data-provenance signal. An app's claimed-vs-verified is a separate axis — a binding the app applies, never a WE term; do not re-type progress into a provenance intent, and do not unify bar+gauge under one "render dimension" (that re-opens the ratified progress ≠ meter ≠ status typing, #1469/#1410). This is a dimension on progress, added under #2533 Fork 2.

Composition

Composes Web Theme (the fill colour resolves from tokens, never an author hex) and the native-first baseline (adopt <progress> verbatim). WE owns this intent contract; FUI ships the rendered block (#1488).
